channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says he is weighing reducing American troop presence in Germany after Iran feud
item.description: President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump suggested he could soon reduce the U.S. military presence in Germany as he continues to feud with Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the U.S-Israel war against Iran.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Austrian nuns who fled their care home are now in Rome and visited the Vatican
item.description: The three octogenarian nuns, who made headlines last year after they broke back into their convent, joined others at St. Peter's Square for a general audience with Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday morning.
item.content:encoded:
The three octogenarian nuns, who made headlines last year after they broke back into their convent, joined others at St. Peter's Square for a general audience with Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday morning.

(Image credit: Joe Klamar)
item.dc:creator: Esme Nicholson

item.title: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
item.description: The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months. In congressional testimony, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not say when the war might end.
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months. In congressional testimony, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth did not say when the war might end.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey Jr.)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home
item.description: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.content:encoded: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: South Korean court sentences ex-President Yoon to 7 years in prison
item.description: An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.
item.content:encoded:
An appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In the midst of an energy crisis, countries make plans to ditch oil, gas and coal
item.description: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.
item.content:encoded:
Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.

(Image credit: Raul Arboleda)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: EU says Meta is failing to keep underage users off Facebook and Instagram
item.description: The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Palestinian refugee uses art to share the story of his childhood with his daughter
item.description: Basel Zaraa was a Palestinian refugee. When his 5-year-old daughter started asking him questions, he told her this story.
item.content:encoded: Basel Zaraa was a Palestinian refugee. When his 5-year-old daughter started asking him questions, he told her this story.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: How the U.K. is reacting to King Charles' visit to the U.S.
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Lord Peter Ricketts, a member of the House of Lords and a former British diplomat, about King Charles' visit to the U.S. during a tumultuous time.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Lord Peter Ricketts, a member of the House of Lords and a former British diplomat, about King Charles' visit to the U.S. during a tumultuous time.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Will UAE's departure from OPEC affect the oil cartel's future influence?
item.description: What are the impacts of the UAE's decision to pull out of the OPEC oil cartel? NPR's A Martinez speaks with Helima Croft, global head of commodity strategy at RBC Capital Markets.
item.content:encoded: What are the impacts of the UAE's decision to pull out of the OPEC oil cartel? NPR's A Martinez speaks with Helima Croft, global head of commodity strategy at RBC Capital Markets.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
